neighborhoods.name: Ribeira / Centro Historico
neighborhoods.description: UNESCO riverside district with colorful buildings, port wine lodges across the river, and iconic Dom Luis bridge
neighborhoods.character: UNESCO riverside, port wine, iconic bridges
neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Porto. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Foz do Douro
neighborhoods.description: Upscale coastal neighborhood at the mouth of the Douro river with beaches, parks, and ocean views
neighborhoods.character: Upscale coastal, river mouth, ocean views

housing.predominantType: Apartments and houses in elegant coastal area

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Porto's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Vila Nova de Gaia
neighborhoods.description: South bank city home to port wine cellars with lower rents and improving Metro connections

housing.predominantType: Apartments and houses at lower Porto prices

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Boavista / Cedofeita
neighborhoods.description: Modern and bohemian neighborhoods around the Casa da Musica with international restaurants and galleries

housing.predominantType: Apartments in renovated and modern buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
